element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns direct control, pheromone mechanics, basic navigation in yard/underground
Player learns recruitment, group control, colony hierarchy and behavior influence
Player learns competitive dynamics, territorial conflict, recruitment against opposition
Player learns resource gathering, pheromone marking, supply chain mechanics
Player learns influence without direct control, delegation, trail maintenance
Player learns group combat tactics, threat assessment, recruitment for defense
Player learns scaling encounters, multi-ant coordination
Player learns environmental threat avoidance, pit mechanics

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Quick Game (visible from start in side-view)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns excavation, 3D spatial reasoning, colony expansion
Player learns environmental chaos, colony resilience, adaptation
Player learns system fragility, need for redundancy, trail re-establishment
Player learns expansion to new map zones, colony life cycle, spatial conquest
Player learns large-scale strategy, multi-front conflict, long-term planning
Player learns human environment penetration, maximum difficulty scenarios

typical_relief_triggers: Successful food discovery and pheromone marking
Eliminating enemy ant colony in a zone
Safe periods between human interference events
typical_tension_triggers: Red ant colony expansion and territorial encroachment
Colony population loss from combat or hazards
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Event-driven with unpredictable hazard interruptions; players must balance proactive colony expansion with reactive crisis management. Quick Game emphasizes tactical moment-to-moment conflict; Full Game emphasizes longer strategic cycles across multiple zones.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Different yard/house zones in Full Game mode offer escalating complexity; SNES scenarios feature distinct environmental hazards unique to each locale

difficulty_curve.early_game: Quick Game introduces core mechanics (ant control, pheromone marking, food gathering) in relatively safe conditions; player learns against one enemy colony with gradually increasing predator pressure
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Full Game begins with single-zone conquest; transitions to multi-zone expansion requiring queen/drone production and long-term planning; introduces house interior as new frontier
difficulty_curve.late_game: Driving red ants and human owners from house; managing multiple colony sites simultaneously; handling increased frequency of major hazards (pesticide, lawnmower, rain flooding)
notable_spikes: First major predator encounter (spider/antlion) before player has established strong recruitment chains
Transition from Quick Game to Full Game's map expansion mechanic
Entry into house interior (SNES scenarios feature scenario-specific hazard combinations)
Pesticide or lawnmower events during vulnerable colony expansion phases

notable_level_patterns.description: Small bounded yard area; two ant colonies compete for same territory; victory determined by complete elimination of opposing colony. Teaches competitive resource denial and direct military mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Quick Game (all instances follow same pattern)

notable_level_patterns.description: Large multi-zone map; player expands by creating young colonies in adjacent zones via queen/drone reproduction. Each zone becomes new battleground; overarching victory requires eliminating all red colonies and evicting humans.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Full Game mode (yard divided into multiple controllable squares)

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight distinct preset scenarios in SNES version, each featuring unique yard layout and environmental hazards specific to locale. Designed as progressive challenge series with escalating complexity.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: SNES Scenario 1-8 (documentation confirms existence but specific scenario details not provided)

notable_level_patterns.description: Experimental Game mode grants player god-like tools (place pheromones, walls, pesticides, creatures) and ability to control red ants/spiders. Removes victory condition; emphasizes emergent simulation behavior and creative play.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Experimental Game (all instances follow same pattern)
